name: White Bean Soup with Escarole
recipeIngredient: 2 cans white cannellini beans (16-ounce each), drained
4 fresh sage leaves, cut into chiffonade
8 ounces escarole or romaine lettuce, cut into chiffonade
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a 4-quart saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add onion and carrots to the pan; stir occasionally until onions become translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. If necessary, add a little water to prevent sticking, then cover and simmer for another 5 minutes.
4. Add garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
5. Stir in one can of beans using a potato masher to mash some beans.
6. Add the remaining beans along with the chicken broth and sage; stir well.
7. Bring to a gentle simmer, then cook until thickened to your desired consistency (about 10 minutes).
8. If too thick, add water; if too thin, reduce liquid by boiling until some evaporates.
9. Stir in escarole or romaine lettuce and cover the pot. Continue simmering for another 5 minutes until the greens are tender.
10. Uncover, stir in parsley, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
11. Serve hot with bread and salad for a complete meal.
Yield: 2 servings
